fields.label: Document event
fields.rationale: The event on the target DocType that triggers the outbound HTTP call. on_update fires on every save; after_insert fires only on first insert. workflow_transition fires on every Workflow state change (regardless of Save / Submit).

fields.label: Condition (Python expression)
fields.rationale: Optional. Python expression evaluated on the target doc at fire time. The webhook fires only when this returns truthy. Example: `doc.amount > 10000`. Empty fires on every matching event.

fields.label: HTTP method
fields.rationale: POST is the canonical outbound-webhook method. GET has no body (webhook_data / webhook_json are sent as query params).

fields.label: Request body shape
fields.rationale: JSON: body is JSON, either built from webhook_data child table or supplied as webhook_json raw template. Form URL-Encoded: body is URL-encoded key/value pairs from webhook_data. Modern APIs almost always want JSON.

fields.label: Custom headers (list of {key, value})
fields.rationale: Optional. JSON list of {key, value} header rows. Content-Type is auto-set per request_structure. Authorization headers (API keys, bearer tokens) go here.

fields.label: Payload key/value pairs (list, used when !webhook_json)
fields.rationale: JSON list of {fieldname, key} pairs. Each row extracts `doc.fieldname` and sends it as `key` in the payload. For simple 1:1 field mapping. Empty when using webhook_json (raw template) instead.

fields.label: Raw JSON template (Jinja over doc)
fields.rationale: Alternative to webhook_data. Jinja-templated raw JSON body. Use when the payload shape does not match a flat key/value mapping (nested objects, arrays, computed fields). Example: '{"invoice": {"id": "{{ doc.name }}", "amount": {{ doc.grand_total }}}}'. Must render to valid JSON at fire time.

fields.label: HMAC signature
fields.rationale: When 1, Frappe signs each request with the webhook_secret via X-Frappe-Webhook-Signature (HMAC-SHA256 of the body). The receiver can verify authenticity. Default 0; enable for any endpoint outside your network.

fields.label: HMAC secret (when enable_security=1)
fields.rationale: Required when enable_security=1. Shared secret used for HMAC-SHA256 signing. Treat as a credential - rotate periodically. Empty when security is disabled.

fields.label: Request timeout (seconds)
fields.rationale: HTTP request timeout. Default 5s is reasonable for most fire-and-forget webhooks. Raise only when the receiver is known to be slow; fast failure is usually preferable to holding a worker for 30s.

fields.label: Background queue
fields.rationale: Frappe fires webhooks as background jobs on the named queue. short (default) is fine for typical webhooks that complete in under a minute. default / long for receivers that take longer.

fields.label: Enabled
fields.rationale: Default enabled. Disabled webhooks stay in the DB but are skipped when the event fires.
